rulings.Case Title: DAVID HILLIARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel- Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff David Hilliard is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell informs Mr. Hilliard of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND ANCHANDO VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Successor For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Petitioner's motion to substitute James Rodriguez as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff Raymond Anchando is granted. James Rodriguez is substituted as successor-in-interest and may amend the caption of the complaint showing such substitution. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 4, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and strict liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages is granted. As to negligence and strict liability, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant could be liable for these products-based claims. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to submit evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant was a seller, manufacturer and/or distributor of asbestos-containing products to which decedent was exposed, whether defendant sold and profited from asbestos-containing products to which decedent was exposed and, if defendant sold such products, whether sales of these asbestos-containing products matched or predominated the relative importance of defendant's contractor services. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
